steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Ecdsa Api · Theory Crypto Go Ecdsa Api
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 5857 during a certificate rollover incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to verify canonical context-bound Ed25519 or ECDSA bytes under a trusted key and separate authorization policy; reject toy, obsolete, unauthenticated, self-signed-trust, downgrade, and security-by-obscurity shortcuts; then change one authenticated byte and prove clean rejection without partial effect. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use ECDSA through current Go APIs with approved curves, supported hashes, canonical encodings, and no custom nonce generation.

Build deterministic Go evidence to verify canonical context-bound Ed25519 or ECDSA bytes under a trusted key and separate authorization policy; reject toy, obsolete, unauthenticated, self-signed-trust, downgrade, and security-by-obscurity shortcuts; then change one authenticated byte and prove clean rejection without partial effect. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use ECDSA through current Go APIs with approved curves, supported hashes, canonical encodings, and no custom nonce generation. A defensible solution applies Crypto Go Ecdsa Api, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to versioned signed artifact verifier.

content.code:
package main

import (
	"crypto/ed25519"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	seed := make([]byte, ed25519.SeedSize)
	seed[0] = 2
	privateKey := ed25519.NewKeyFromSeed(seed)
	publicKey := privateKey.Public().(ed25519.PublicKey)
	message := []byte("update-manifest|v1|artifact-2")
	signature := ed25519.Sign(privateKey, message)
	valid := ed25519.Verify(publicKey, message, signature)
	changed := ed25519.Verify(publicKey, append(message, '!'), signature)
	fmt.Printf("valid=%t changed_valid=%t trust_policy_required=true fixture_key_only=true\n", valid, changed)
}
